refractory hepatic hydrothorax

PROCEDURE

Indwelling Tunneled Pleural Catheter Placement (Left)
Thoracic Ultrasound
Indwelling Tunneled Pleural Catheter Placement (Right)

ANESTHESIA Local anesthesia (Lidocaine 1%)

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Chest Ultrasound Findings

Hemithorax: Left
Findings: Pleural effusion identified and site marked for procedure.

Catheter Placement The left chest was prepped and draped in sterile fashion. Local anesthesia was infiltrated at the insertion site. A finder needle was used to enter the pleural space; pleural fluid was aspirated to confirm location. The needle was withdrawn and a guidewire was placed into the pleural space.

Two 5 mm skin incisions were made at the guidewire entry site, as well as 5-6 cm away. The tunneling device was used to tunnel the catheter in the subcutaneous tissue. Serial dilation over the guidewire was then achieved. The introducer sheath was placed and the guidewire removed.

The tunneled pleural catheter was then placed into the pleural space via the introducer sheath. The pleural entry site was closed with an absorbable suture. The catheter was secured to the skin using 2-0 nylon. Sterile dressings were applied.

PROCEDURE FINDINGS

Appearance: Drained

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] presents with a 14 mm LLL pulmonary nodule requiring bronchoscopic diagnosis and staging requiring histopathologic diagnosis.

CONSENT The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy and cryobiopsy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

LLL pulmonary nodule, 14 mm

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

LLL pulmonary nodule, 14 mm

PROCEDURE

Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to LLL target
Radial EBUS localization (concentric view)
Transbronchial Cryobiopsy (LLL)
Cone-beam CT imaging with confirmation
TBNA of LLL target (4 passes)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]
INSTRUMENTATION Ion robotic bronchoscopy platform; biopsy forceps; cone-beam CT system.

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Robotic Navigational Bronchoscopy (Ion) — LLL Nodule A CT-based navigation plan was loaded and registration was completed. The robotic catheter was advanced to the planned LLL target (14 mm).

A CBCT/CACT spin was obtained intra-procedurally.
Image fusion was performed to align intra-op imaging with the pre-procedure CT. The updated overlay demonstrated Cone Beam CT.
The patient tolerated the imaging without complications.

Tool-in-lesion was confirmed with Cone Beam CT.

Localization: Radial EBUS was performed.
Target Confirmation: rEBUS demonstrated concentric view of the lesion.
LLL Target Sampling

TBNA: Transbronchial needle aspiration was performed. A total of 4 passes were obtained from the LLL target.

Transbronchial Cryobiopsy Attention was turned to the Left Lower Lobe (LLL) for transbronchial cryobiopsy.

A cryoprobe was utilized for sampling.

Findings & Hemostasis

Yield: A total of 3 samples were collected.
Hemostasis:
Final airway inspection was performed.

Pneumothorax Check: Post-procedure assessment for pneumothorax was performed.

SPECIMENS

LLL Transbronchial Cryobiopsy (3 samples) — Histology

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Successful transbronchial cryobiopsy of LLL for diagnostic evaluation of peripheral lung lesion.

No pneumothorax or significant bleeding complications.

Recover per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging to assess for late pneumothorax per local workflow.

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION The patient presents with complicated parapneumonic effusion right side.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to the procedure were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

complicated parapneumonic effusion right side

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

complicated parapneumonic effusion right side
Successful drainage of 450 mL purulent fluid

PROCEDURE

Pigtail Catheter Placement

ATTENDING [Name]

SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Pigtail Catheter Placement (Modified for Drainage) The insertion site was prepped and draped in sterile fashion. Anesthesia: Lidocaine 1% was used for local anesthesia. Entry Site: Right hemithorax.
Findings and Technique: A 14Fr pigtail catheter was utilized. A pigtail catheter was inserted using the Seldinger technique. Entry into the pleural space was confirmed with the easy removal of pleural fluid. A guidewire was inserted using the introducer needle. The introducer needle was then removed. A dilator was then inserted over the wire with a twisting motion in order to form a tract for catheter insertion. The dilator was removed and the pigtail catheter (with trocar) was advanced over the guidewire.

Drainage:

Fluid Removed: 450 mL
Appearance: Purulent
Intrapleural fibrinolytic therapy was documented using tPA + DNase (tPA 10.0 mg, DNase 10.0 mg), schedule q12h, planned doses 3.

The catheter was secured and sterile dressings were applied.

The patient tolerated the procedure well without immediate complications.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Right pleural effusion successfully drained via pigtail catheter.

Total of 450 mL purulent fluid removed.

Post-procedure monitoring per protocol.

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION The patient presents with 2 hours.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to the procedure were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

2 hours

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

2 hours

Status post left tunneled pleural catheter placement

PROCEDURE

Indwelling Tunneled Pleural Catheter Placement (Left)
Thoracic Ultrasound

ANESTHESIA Moderate sedation

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After adequate sedation was achieved, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Therapeutic injection was performed with talc slurry instilled through catheter and clamped for (total dose 5g).

Chest Ultrasound Findings

Hemithorax: Left
Findings: Pleural effusion identified and site marked for procedure.

Catheter Placement The left chest was prepped and draped in sterile fashion. Local anesthesia was infiltrated at the insertion site. A finder needle was used to enter the pleural space; pleural fluid was aspirated to confirm location. The needle was withdrawn and a guidewire was placed into the pleural space.

Two 5 mm skin incisions were made at the guidewire entry site, as well as 5-6 cm away. The tunneling device was used to tunnel the catheter in the subcutaneous tissue. Serial dilation over the guidewire was then achieved. The introducer sheath was placed and the guidewire removed.

The PleurX catheter was then placed into the pleural space via the introducer sheath. The pleural entry site was closed with an absorbable suture. The catheter was secured to the skin using 2-0 nylon. Sterile dressings were applied.

PROCEDURE FINDINGS

Fluid Removed: 1200 mL (1.2 L)

Pleurodesis: Chemical pleurodesis was performed via the tunneled pleural catheter using Talc (dose: 5.0 g) with method Chemical - slurry.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Successful placement of Left Indwelling Tunneled Pleural Catheter (PleurX).

1.2 L of pleural fluid drained during procedure.

Catheter instructions and drainage kit provided to patient.

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] presents with RML 2 requiring histopathologic diagnosis.

CONSENT The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy and cryobiopsy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

RML pulmonary nodule, 25 mm

Mediastinal/Hilar lymphadenopathy (suspected)

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

RML pulmonary nodule, 25 mm

PROCEDURE

Linear Endobronchial Ultrasound (EBUS) with Transbronchial Needle Aspiration (TBNA)
Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to RML target
Radial EBUS localization (eccentric view)
Transbronchial Cryobiopsy (RML)
Cone-beam CT imaging with confirmation
Transbronchial needle aspiration of RML target (5 passes)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]

INSTRUMENTATION Ion robotic bronchoscopy platform; rEBUS probe; biopsy forceps; cone-beam CT system.

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Robotic Navigational Bronchoscopy (Ion) — RML Nodule A CT-based navigation plan was loaded and registration was completed. The robotic catheter was advanced to the planned RML target (25 mm).

A CBCT/CACT spin was obtained intra-procedurally.
Image fusion was performed to align intra-op imaging with the pre-procedure CT. The updated overlay demonstrated Cone Beam CT.
The patient tolerated the imaging without complications.

Localization: Radial EBUS was performed.
Target Confirmation: rEBUS demonstrated eccentric view of the lesion. Cone-beam CT imaging: Tool-in-lesion was confirmed by CBCT.
RML Target Sampling

TBNA: Transbronchial needle aspiration was performed. A total of 5 passes were obtained from the RML target.

Transbronchial Cryobiopsy Attention was turned to the Right Middle Lobe (RML) for transbronchial cryobiopsy.

A 1.1 mm cryoprobe was utilized for sampling.

Findings & Hemostasis

Yield: A total of 3 samples were collected.
Hemostasis:
Final airway inspection was performed.

Pneumothorax Check: Post-procedure assessment for pneumothorax was performed.

EBUS-TBNA Staging The endobronchial ultrasound-capable bronchoscope was introduced and a systematic mediastinal and hilar survey was performed. The following lymph node stations were identified and sampled based on ultrasound criteria using a 22G, 25G needle:

Station 7 (Subcarinal): The node measured 8.4 mm by EBUS. 5 passes were performed. Biopsy Tools: TBNA. ROSE Results: Adequate lymphocytes.

Station 4R (Right Lower Paratracheal): The node measured 6.1 mm by EBUS. 4 passes were performed. Biopsy Tools: TBNA. ROSE Results: Adequate lymphocytes.

Prior to withdrawal of the bronchoscope, inspection demonstrated no evidence of bleeding. The patient tolerated the procedure well. There were no immediate complications.

SPECIMENS

RML Transbronchial Cryobiopsy (3 samples) — Histology

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Successful transbronchial cryobiopsy of RML for diagnostic evaluation of peripheral lung lesion.

No pneumothorax or significant bleeding complications.

Recover per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging to assess for late pneumothorax per local workflow.

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] presents with pleural effusion requiring histopathologic diagnosis.

CONSENT The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy and cryobiopsy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

pleural effusion

Mediastinal/Hilar lymphadenopathy (suspected)

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

pleural effusion

PROCEDURE

Right Medical Thoracoscopy (Diagnostic)
Evacuation of pleural fluid (800 mL)
Pleural biopsies (4 specimens)
Talc poudrage pleurodesis
Chest tube placement (24 Fr)
Linear Endobronchial Ultrasound (EBUS) with Transbronchial Needle Aspiration (TBNA)
Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to RLL target
Radial EBUS localization (concentric view)
Transbronchial Cryobiopsy (RLL)
Cone-beam CT imaging with confirmation
Transbronchial needle aspiration of RLL target (5 passes)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]

INSTRUMENTATION Ion robotic bronchoscopy platform; rEBUS probe; biopsy forceps; cone-beam CT system.

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Robotic Navigational Bronchoscopy (Ion) — RLL Nodule A CT-based navigation plan was loaded and registration was completed. The robotic catheter was advanced to the planned RLL target (28 mm).

A CBCT/CACT spin was obtained intra-procedurally.
Image fusion was performed to align intra-op imaging with the pre-procedure CT. The updated overlay demonstrated Cone Beam CT.
The patient tolerated the imaging without complications.

Localization: Radial EBUS was performed.
Target Confirmation: rEBUS demonstrated concentric view of the lesion. Cone-beam CT imaging: Tool-in-lesion was confirmed by CBCT.
RLL Target Sampling

TBNA: Transbronchial needle aspiration was performed. A total of 5 passes were obtained from the RLL target.

Transbronchial Cryobiopsy Attention was turned to the Right Lower Lobe (RLL) for transbronchial cryobiopsy.

A cryoprobe was utilized for sampling.

Findings & Hemostasis

Yield: A total of 4 samples were collected.
Hemostasis:
Final airway inspection was performed.

Pneumothorax Check: Post-procedure assessment for pneumothorax was performed.

EBUS-TBNA Staging The endobronchial ultrasound-capable bronchoscope was introduced and a systematic mediastinal and hilar survey was performed. The following lymph node stations were identified and sampled based on ultrasound criteria using a 22G, 25G needle:

Station 7 (Subcarinal): The node measured 8.2 mm by EBUS. 5 passes were performed. Biopsy Tools: TBNA. ROSE Results: Malignant.

Prior to withdrawal of the bronchoscope, inspection demonstrated no evidence of bleeding. The patient tolerated the procedure well. There were no immediate complications.

Right Diagnostic Thoracoscopy The chest was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.

Survey: Thoracoscopic survey performed.

Intervention: Evacuation of pleural fluid (800 mL).
Intervention: Pleural biopsies (4 specimens).
Intervention: Talc poudrage pleurodesis.
Intervention: Chest tube placement (24 Fr).

The patient tolerated the procedure well. There were no immediate complications.

SPECIMENS

RLL Transbronchial Cryobiopsy (4 samples) — Histology

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Successful transbronchial cryobiopsy of RLL for diagnostic evaluation of peripheral lung lesion.

No pneumothorax or significant bleeding complications.

Recover per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging to assess for late pneumothorax per local workflow.

TBNA: Transbronchial needle aspiration was performed. A total of 3 passes were obtained from the LLL target.

Final airway inspection was performed.

malignant pleural effusion

malignant pleural effusion

Status post right tunneled pleural catheter placement

PROCEDURE

Indwelling Tunneled Pleural Catheter Placement (Right)
Thoracic Ultrasound

ANESTHESIA Local anesthesia (Lidocaine 1%)

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Chest Ultrasound Findings

Hemithorax: Right
Findings: Pleural effusion identified and site marked for procedure.

Catheter Placement The right chest was prepped and draped in sterile fashion. Local anesthesia was infiltrated at the insertion site. A finder needle was used to enter the pleural space; pleural fluid was aspirated to confirm location. The needle was withdrawn and a guidewire was placed into the pleural space.

Two 5 mm skin incisions were made at the guidewire entry site, as well as 5-6 cm away. The tunneling device was used to tunnel the catheter in the subcutaneous tissue. Serial dilation over the guidewire was then achieved. The introducer sheath was placed and the guidewire removed.

The Other catheter was then placed into the pleural space via the introducer sheath. The pleural entry site was closed with an absorbable suture. The catheter was secured to the skin using 2-0 nylon. Sterile dressings were applied.

PROCEDURE FINDINGS

Fluid Removed: 500 mL (0.5 L)

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Successful placement of Right Indwelling Tunneled Pleural Catheter.

0.5 L of pleural fluid drained during procedure.

Catheter instructions and drainage kit provided to patient.
